metadata.name: Treasure Cruise cast via Delve — exile 7 graveyard cards, draw 3
metadata.description: p1 has 7 cards in their graveyard (Forest, Island, Mountain, Plains, Swamp, Lightning Bolt, Counterspell) from earlier play. p1 casts Treasure Cruise ({7}{U}, Sorcery, Delve) by exiling all 7 graveyard cards via Delve to pay the {7} generic, then paying {U} from the mana pool. After both players pass priority, Treasure Cruise resolves and p1 draws 3 cards. The script verifies: (1) 7 cards in p1 graveyard and Treasure Cruise in hand before casting, (2) all 7 graveyard cards move to exile and Treasure Cruise leaves hand at cast time, (3) after resolution the stack is empty, (4) p1 draws 3 cards (hand increases from 3 to 6 after TC leaves hand), and (5) p1 life remains 40.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.66a: Delve is a static ability that functions while the spell is on the stack. 'For each generic mana in this spell's total cost, you may exile a card from your graveyard rather than pay that mana.' Treasure Cruise total cost is {7}{U}. p1 exiles all 7 graveyard cards to pay the entire {7} generic component, then pays {U} from the mana pool for the colored pip. CR 702.66b: Delve is not an additional or alternative cost — it is applied after the total cost is determined at cast time (CR 601.2). The 7 exiled graveyard cards (Forest, Island, Mountain, Plains, Swamp, Lightning Bolt, Counterspell) move to exile as part of paying the casting cost; they are NOT in the graveyard at the point of resolution. The graveyard cards chosen are basic lands and iconic instants — all available in the engine's card pool. Initial hand: 4 cards (Treasure Cruise + Island x3 as filler), so after TC is cast (hand = 3) and 3 cards are drawn (hand = 6), we assert hand count = 6. Library has 3 Islands to enable the draw-3 effect without running out of cards. p1 has {U} in the mana pool from a tapped Island to pay the colored pip. Assertions: graveyard starts at 7 cards; after casting, graveyard goes to 0 and exile contains all 7 cards; after resolution, hand count is 6 and stack is empty. p1 life stays at 40 (Treasure Cruise has no life-loss effect). CR 608.2: Sorcery resolves; draw 3 cards effect executes; card moves to p1 graveyard.

script.step_note: p1 casts Treasure Cruise using Delve — exiling all 7 graveyard cards to pay the {7} generic, paying only {U} from the mana pool. After both players pass priority, Treasure Cruise resolves and p1 draws 3 cards.

actions.description: Initial state: 7 cards in p1 graveyard, Treasure Cruise in p1 hand (4 cards total), stack empty, p1 at 40 life
actions.note: 7 cards are in p1's graveyard representing prior play. Treasure Cruise is in hand alongside 3 filler Islands. p1 has {U} in pool from a previously tapped Island.

CR 702.66a: Delve — p1 exiles 7 cards from graveyard (Forest, Island, Mountain, Plains, Swamp, Lightning Bolt, Counterspell) to pay the {7} generic component of Treasure Cruise's {7}{U} cost. CR 601.2: p1 pays {U} from the mana pool for the colored pip. CR 702.66b: Delve is not an additional or alternative cost; it applies after the total cost is determined.

actions.description: After casting: Treasure Cruise on stack, p1 hand has 3 cards, p1 graveyard is empty (all 7 cards moved to exile via Delve)
actions.note: CR 702.66a: The 7 graveyard cards were exiled as payment at cast time — they are in exile, not the graveyard. Treasure Cruise is on the stack. Hand now has 3 cards (the 3 Islands).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority in succession. The stack has one object — Treasure Cruise resolves.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Treasure Cruise resolves. p1 draws 3 cards from the top of their library.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Sorcery resolves; draw-3 effect executes; Treasure Cruise moves to p1's graveyard.

actions.description: After resolution: stack empty, p1 hand has 6 cards (3 Islands + 3 drawn), Treasure Cruise in p1 graveyard, p1 life unchanged at 40
actions.note: p1 drew 3 cards: hand went from 3 (after casting TC) to 6. Treasure Cruise itself moved to p1's graveyard after resolving. The 7 Delve-exiled cards remain in exile permanently. p1 life is 40 — Treasure Cruise has no life-loss effect.
